reforms.description: The Mexican government has announced the establishment of a Climate Fund under the General Law on Climate Change to collect and channel public and private resources for climate-change interventions, while also committing to raise R&D intensity to 1% of GDP to strengthen public funding for the business sector and enhance the innovation environment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance funding for innovation and climate change, which are expected to raise productivity and support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been officially announced but are not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: Both reforms represent significant structural changes in funding mechanisms for climate and R&D.
reforms.source_quote: The General Law on Climate Change establishes the creation of a Climate Fund for the collection and channelling of public and private resources for mitigation and adaptation actions.

reforms.description: The adoption of the PPP Law in 2012 enhances private investment through public-private partnerships, aiming to improve infrastructure financing and efficiency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform is expected to stimulate private investment and improve infrastructure, which can lead to increased economic activity and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The PPP Law has been successfully adopted and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The PPP Law represents a major reform in how infrastructure projects are financed and executed in Mexico.
reforms.source_quote: The adoption of the PPP Law in 2012 is another step towards enhancing private investment through public-private partnerships.
